summary: Reworked src/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c to make HSD_ByteCodeEval substantially closer to the target by changing local variable types/names, operand decoding state, stack value access through ByteCodeVal, math helper usage, and panic/report call shapes. The decomp.dev report showed HSD_ByteCodeEval improving from 99.08% to 99.92% matched, with .sdata also improving, but .sdata2 regressed by 7 bytes.

key_files.role: Main HSD_ByteCodeEval rewrite/refinement; 165 added and 180 deleted lines.
Adjusted HSD_ByteCodeEval prototype nb_args from u32 to int.
Moved/shared DEG_TO_RAD and RAD_TO_DEG macro definitions.

what_changed: Included baselib/util.h and MSL/math_ppc.h in bytecode.c.
Changed ByteCodeVal.i from s32 to int.
Renamed HSD_ByteCodeEval parameter in the implementation from pc to bytecode and changed nb_args type from u32 to int, mirrored in bytecode.h.
Replaced extra_bytes/opcode-style operand parsing with operand_count/last_command, while preserving the multi-byte operand decoding loop.
Reworked many integer stack operations to assign through ((ByteCodeVal*) &stack->data)->i instead of repeated direct void* casts to s32.
Introduced local temporaries f0/f1 and d0/d1 to model top-of-stack and next-stack operands for float and integer opcodes.
Replaced a hand-expanded sqrt approximation using __frsqrte with sqrtf for opcode 0x16.
Renamed local fmod helper to fmodf and changed its formula to use __fabsf and a long long quotient expression.
Moved DEG_TO_RAD and RAD_TO_DEG constants out of bytecode.c into util.h.
Changed atan2 angle handling to use fabsf_bitwise(f0) and RAD_TO_DEG from util.h.
Changed HSD_Panic calls from string literal filename and NULL message to HSD_Panic(__FILE__, line, "").
Fixed the unexpected opcode OSReport call shape to pass last_command as the 0x%x argument.
smart_moves: Using ByteCodeVal consistently for stack->data made int/float punning explicit and likely helped the compiler choose target-like loads/stores.
Separating f0/f1 and d0/d1 temporaries mirrors stack-machine operand order and gives the compiler more target-like register lifetimes than generic fv/a/b variables.
Changing nb_args to int aligned the public prototype with the implementation’s assert comparison and decomp expectations.
Replacing the custom square-root Newton refinement with sqrtf reduced handwritten code and matched the desired library call/codegen more closely.
Moving RAD_TO_DEG/DEG_TO_RAD into util.h reduced local .sdata2 ownership in bytecode.c, though the bot report indicates this also caused a small .sdata2 match regression.
Using __FILE__ and empty-string panic messages matched the observed panic call pattern better than hardcoded "bytecode.c" plus NULL.
decomp_lessons: For stack-machine evaluators stored in generic pointer fields, try a small union such as ByteCodeVal and write through the relevant union member instead of bouncing between void* and scalar casts.

Local variable names do not matter semantically but their grouping and reuse patterns can strongly influence register allocation and matching; introduce operand-specific temporaries when a large switch is nearly matched.
When matching math-heavy baselib code, test whether target code used MSL functions such as sqrtf/powf/atan2f instead of manually expanded PPC approximations.
Header prototype signedness can matter for matching when values are compared against signed locals or assertions.
Shared constants can improve one section while regressing another; always check decomp.dev section-level reports, not only the main function percentage.
Panic/report helper argument spelling, including __FILE__ versus string literals and NULL versus empty string, can affect matching and data sections.
naming_conventions: pc was renamed to bytecode in the implementation for clarity, while the header still names the parameter pc.
Integer temporaries a/b became d1/d0, and float temporaries used f0/f1/fv.
The local fmod helper was renamed fmodf, matching float-specific library naming style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use int instead of s32/u32 where the target appears to have signed integer comparisons or stack boolean results.
Use ((ByteCodeVal*) &stack->data)->i and ->f lvalue assignments to shape stores into the HSD_SList data field.
For boolean opcodes, assign the comparison result into ByteCodeVal.i rather than casting the result directly to void*.
Use a u8 last_command loaded before the switch to preserve the opcode value for later operand handling and error reporting.
Use sqrtf and include MSL/math_ppc.h rather than keeping an inline __frsqrte sequence when trying to match this bytecode evaluator.
Use fabsf_bitwise for zero checks in atan2-degree logic to avoid ordinary fabsf codegen when bitwise absolute value is target-like.
Prefer HSD_Panic(__FILE__, line, "") in this file’s matching context.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report: HSD_ByteCodeEval improved by +47 bytes from 99.08% to 99.92%; .sdata improved by +4 bytes from 54.55% to 76.92%; .sdata2 had one broken match, -7 bytes from 100.00% to 87.50%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching HSD_ByteCodeEval or repair the bytecode .sdata2 regression from PR 2167.
Search for other uses of fabsf_bitwise and RAD_TO_DEG in baselib math-heavy decomp work.
Check whether util.h DEG_TO_RAD/RAD_TO_DEG placement caused any subsequent data-section cleanup.
Compare later bytecode.c revisions for final naming of last_command, operand_count, f0/f1, and d0/d1.
